observations: Round-1 HIGH (entries 5+9 simultaneously bypassed by a meaning-reversing paraphrase) is CONFIRMED FIXED for the exact demonstrated vector: re-ran this evaluator's own exact round-1 kill-shot fixture ('Track 2 is NOT candidate scope for a subsequent phase; it remains the recommended selection for phase 005 going forward, activated through the standard planning lifecycle.') against the rebuilt entry 9 -- now correctly FAILS ('exact pinned replacement sentence not found'). A fully compliant fixture containing the exact pinned sentence (single physical line) correctly PASSES; a line-wrapped variant of the same compliant sentence (split across three markdown lines, exercising the line-join tolerance) also correctly PASSES. A 'belt-and-suspenders' fixture containing BOTH the genuine pinned sentence AND a second, separate sentence re-asserting 'Track 2 is still... the recommended path' is independently caught by layer (b)'s remainder co-occurrence guard specifically (confirmed: the AssertionError fires on the remainder-guard line, not the pinned-sentence assertion, which passes) -- proving layer (b) is genuinely load-bearing, not merely redundant with layer (a). Also confirmed no false-positive risk: a remainder mentioning 'recommended' in a wholly unrelated context ('The recommended reading order for new contributors...') without co-occurring 'Track 2' correctly still PASSES.

Round-1 LOW (entry 10 cannot detect an unauthorized third DEBT row) is CONFIRMED FIXED by the new, appended entry 22: independently re-proven in a disposable scratch git clone of the real repository (HEAD ada3b9c) across all three required states -- pre-implementation (delta=0, 18 pre-existing real NDEBT rows at HEAD, confirmed via live git show) correctly FAILS; the compliant two-new-row state (delta=2) correctly PASSES; an unauthorized third-row state (NDEBT-019/020/021, delta=3) correctly FAILS. The HEAD-anchoring is confirmed sound: it does not false-fail against the 18 genuinely pre-existing rows, only asserting the DELTA equals exactly 2.

MODE A'S TWO DISCLOSED RESIDUAL VECTORS -- independently reproduced by this evaluator (not merely trusted from Mode A's transcript) and judged on their own merits, per this dispatch's explicit instruction: (1) PREFIX-NEGATION -- 'It is no longer true that <pinned sentence verbatim>' -- correctly still PASSES entry 9 (the pinned substring is trivially contained within the negated sentence). (2) SYNONYM-REVERSAL -- the pinned sentence followed by a second sentence using 'preferred' instead of 'recommended' ('Track 2 is the preferred phase-005 selection for teams that want enforcement closure sooner') -- correctly still PASSES (layer (b)'s co-occurrence guard only scans for the literal token 'recommended', not synonyms). Both vectors are real and confirmed, exactly as Mode A disclosed -- this evaluator does not dispute their existence. JUDGMENT (this evaluator's own, independent of Mode A's reasoning): ACCEPTED AS NON-BLOCKING RESIDUAL, concurring with Mode A's disposition, for the following reasons found independently persuasive: (a) unlike the round-1 finding (where the CORRECT claim could be entirely ABSENT while all checks passed), layer (a) now GUARANTEES the true, correct sentence is genuinely present verbatim in the section -- a materially stronger floor than round-1's gap; (b) both residual vectors require the generator to author an ADDITIONAL sentence/clause beyond the single pinned replacement, which independently and directly violates this contract's own explicit scope text ('No other ROADMAP.md sentence, section, or line is edited', scope.files_modify[1].purpose) -- a distinct, separately-catchable scope violation, not merely a clever restatement within the one authorized edit; (c) this mirrors the NDEBT-014 item-5 lesson's own already-accepted residual class in this codebase (natural-language paraphrase-mirroring risk that persists AFTER a closed-set exact-text pin is applied to the core claim is a known, tolerated residual -- e.g. the guide-card anti-scope-creep check in contract 045 carries an analogous, accepted paraphrase-bypass residual); (d) a full-section-or-file SHA pin (the alternative that would fully close this) is disproportionate here, unlike 045's single-feature-owned checklist -- ROADMAP.md is a living, cross-feature document every future feature may also edit, and even a Sequencing-Recommendation-section-only SHA pin would need to be re-derived and re-pinned by every subsequent feature that ever touches this section, a maintenance burden out of proportion to the residual's severity. NON-BLOCKING RECOMMENDATIONS (not required for approval): (i) design_notes.paraphrase_bypass_fix currently does NOT disclose these two vectors anywhere in the contract's own durable text -- only Mode A's out-of-band dispatch message disclosed them to this evaluator; per this codebase's own consistent transparency practice (e.g. NDEBT-014's and NDEBT-016's own disclosed limitations, and this same contract's honest disclosure of the entry-6/entry-15 HEAD-caret timing caveats), this evaluator recommends the Generator append a brief, honest disclosure of both vectors to design_notes.paraphrase_bypass_fix for durable audit-trail transparency, the next time this contract is touched -- a documentation-only addition, not a functional change. (ii) if full closure is ever desired, a proportionate (not disproportionate) HEAD-anchored fix exists: bound the Sequencing Recommendation section's own line-joined text to a small edit-distance/length-delta from HEAD's own section text (analogous to entry 22's row-count-delta pattern, applied to this section rather than the whole file), which would catch any added sentence without requiring a whole-file or whole-section byte-exact pin.

Per-entry stability vs revision 1 independently confirmed: re-ran all 22 verification entries live against the real, current repository and reproduced the exact expected pre-implementation pattern (entries 2, 5, 7, 9, 10, 11, 13, 22 FAIL; the remaining 14 PASS -- entry 22 joining the fail set is expected and correct, since it is new and pre-implementation). Direct text comparison of entries 1-8 and 10-21 against this evaluator's own retained revision-1 record confirms byte-identical commands and acceptance_test text throughout (spot-verified in full for entries 10 and 14, the two entries closest to this round's changes, plus visual comparison of all others) -- only entry 9's command changed (as disclosed) and entry 22 was newly appended (not inserted mid-array, preserving entries 1-21's indices, confirmed by direct index-by-index re-read). Entry 14 (the round-0 BLOCKER fix) was independently regression-checked fresh in this round, in a newly-reset scratch git clone: the compliant post-implementation state (spec status flipped) correctly PASSES; injecting an unauthorized tracked edit to .agent/contracts/044.json correctly FAILS with the same diagnostic as before; reverting restores PASS -- no regression from this revision's unrelated entry-9/entry-22 changes.

Semantic-correctness and factual-accuracy check of the pinned replacement sentence, performed by directly reading the real, current docs/planning/ROADMAP.md: the real supersession note (the '## Track 2 -- [SUPERSEDED as the phase-005 selection, 2026-07-17] ...' heading and its blockquote, confirmed at the file's own ~95-102 line range) states, in its own words, 'phase 005 was activated as the Ecosystem Engineering Cycle ... The enforcement-closure items below are NOT the phase-005 scope; they remain candidate scope for a subsequent phase' -- this is semantically consistent with, and in fact near-verbatim echoed by, the pinned replacement sentence ('Track 2 was superseded as the phase-005 selection by NIP-0001 ... and remains candidate scope for a subsequent phase'). The real stale sentence being replaced was independently confirmed at its own real location (docs/planning/ROADMAP.md's '## Sequencing Recommendation' section, real line ~168: 'Track 2 is the recommended phase 005, activated through the standard planning lifecycle.') -- an exact match to the sentence scope.files_modify[1].purpose names for replacement. The pinned sentence's parenthetical '(see the supersession note above)' is confirmed positionally accurate: the supersession note (line ~95-102) genuinely precedes the Sequencing Recommendation section (line ~166) in the real file. The pinned sentence is therefore judged semantically correct and factually true of the real plan of record, not merely internally consistent with the contract's own prose.

Full untracked-aware git status check on the real repository, performed before, during, and after this round's review, shows no change attributable to this review beyond this artifact's own overwrite: only the planner's pre-existing .agent/feature_list_005.json edit, the pre-existing untracked .agent/contracts/046.json and .agent/planning_notes_046.md, and the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md are present; git diff --stat HEAD against every tracked file this review discusses is empty. All fixture and adversarial-direction testing was performed exclusively in a disposable git clone of the real repository under this evaluator's own session scratchpad (never a real repository path), with cwd explicitly verified via an exact-path string comparison before every destructive command (rm/git checkout) issued in that scratch clone. No git tag was created or deleted anywhere (real repository tag count unchanged); no sign-off was simulated.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 2 (round 1 of this evaluator's own review). Both findings from this evaluator's round-0 review are confirmed genuinely fixed: (1) the HIGH finding (entries 5+9 both bypassed by a meaning-reversing paraphrase) is closed for the exact demonstrated vector -- this evaluator's own kill-shot fixture, re-run verbatim against the rebuilt entry 9, now correctly fails; a compliant fixture and a line-wrapped variant of it both correctly pass; a belt-and-suspenders fixture (pinned sentence present plus a second reversing sentence) is independently caught by the new layer-(b) remainder co-occurrence guard, proving that layer is genuinely load-bearing, not decorative. (2) the LOW finding (entry 10 cannot bound total DEBT-row count) is closed by the new, appended entry 22, independently re-proven across all three required states (pre-implementation FAILS, compliant two-row state PASSES, unauthorized third-row state FAILS) with HEAD-anchoring confirmed not to false-fail against the real, pre-existing 18 NDEBT rows. This evaluator independently reproduced Mode A's two disclosed residual vectors (prefix-negation, synonym-reversal via 'preferred') -- both are real and confirmed to still pass entry 9 -- and, exercising its own independent judgment as the finder of the original gap, concurs with Mode A's non-blocking disposition: layer (a) now guarantees the correct sentence's genuine presence (a materially stronger floor than the entirely-absent-correct-content gap round 1 found), both residual vectors require an additional authored sentence that independently violates this contract's own explicit 'no other sentence is edited' scope text (a distinct, separately-catchable violation, not a clever restatement within the one authorized edit), the residual class mirrors an already-accepted NDEBT-014-item-5 paraphrase-mirroring precedent elsewhere in this same codebase, and a full section/file SHA pin would be a disproportionate tool for a living, cross-feature document. Two non-blocking recommendations are recorded for the Generator's discretion, not required for approval: honestly disclosing both residual vectors in design_notes.paraphrase_bypass_fix for durable transparency (currently only disclosed out-of-band by Mode A to this evaluator), and, if full closure is ever desired, a proportionate HEAD-anchored section-length-delta bound (mirroring entry 22's own pattern) rather than a disproportionate whole-section SHA pin. Per-entry stability is confirmed: all 22 verification entries independently re-run live reproduce the exact expected pre-implementation pattern (2, 5, 7, 9, 10, 11, 13, 22 FAIL; remaining 14 PASS); entries 1-8 and 10-21 are byte-identical to revision 1; only entry 9 changed and entry 22 was appended (not inserted, preserving all prior indices); entry 14 (the round-0 BLOCKER fix) shows no regression, independently re-confirmed in both required directions in a fresh scratch clone. The pinned replacement sentence is independently confirmed semantically correct and factually true against the real ROADMAP.md's own supersession note and the real stale sentence's own location. This contract is fit for implementation as-is. next_state: advance to Loop-2 (@generator implements the contracted scope; @validator Mode B verifies the implementation against this approved contract; this evaluator then runs the post-code QA verdict).
